dissection.title: The trade‑off between faster access and evidentiary certainty in the FDA’s Accelerated Approval of cancer drugs: empirical evidence on patient preferences and regulatory decision‑making

findings.text: Individuals with experience of cancer may be willing to wait longer for FDA approval
findings.evidence: individuals with experience of cancer may be willing to wait longer for FDA approval

findings.text: Patients are most likely to accept greater uncertainty only when no treatment alternatives exist
findings.evidence: patients are most likely to accept greater uncertainty only when no treatment alternatives exist

findings.text: Empirical evidence in support of claims that clinical or non-clinical factors influence FDA approval decisions is limited
findings.evidence: empirical evidence in support of claims that clinical or non-clinical factors influence FDA approval decisions is limited

findings.text: FDA conversion–withdrawal decisions diverge from mandated post-marketing study outcomes
findings.evidence: many FDA conversion–withdrawal decisions diverge from mandated post-marketing study outcomes

notes: Methods section is partially present in the supplied text
